preamble: The shift from the voiced aspirated bilabial stop [bʱ] to the labio-velar approximant [w] is a form of extreme lenition. It typically occurs in intervocalic positions where the stop closure is completely lost, leaving only the labial glide quality.

examples.note: In Sylheti and some Bengali dialects, standard voiced aspirates weaken to glides or fricatives.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
